2026-03-19 Reuters Tokyo stocks BOJ Micron oil broad selloff relative strength

snapshot: At 11:00 JST the Nikkei was 53,744.86, down 1,494.54. On the Prime market, 42 stocks were up and 1,529 were down, leaving the tape broadly negative.
Chicago Nikkei 225 futures were 53,465, down 1,445 versus the Osaka day session. The overnight futures gap is still weighing on cash trade.
USD/JPY was 159.66 at 11:00 JST, while one-week implied volatility moved into the 11% range. BOJ event risk is keeping FX volatility elevated.
WTI settled at $96.32, but after-hours trading briefly reached $100.55. That is still supportive for resource names.
Only mining and shipping were positive among the 33 Prime sectors. This remains a relative-strength stock-picking market rather than an index dip-buying market.

drivers.driver: Post-FOMC U.S. equity shock and renewed oil pressure
drivers.why_today: The Dow fell 768 points, the U.S. 10-year rose to 4.261%, and USD/JPY stayed in the upper 159s. WTI briefly reclaimed $100 after hours, leaving a clear headwind for the broad index.

drivers.driver: Only mining and shipping are still up
drivers.why_today: As of 11:00 JST, only two of the 33 Prime sectors were positive. Capital is being forced into resources and stock-specific relative strength rather than broad-market rebound trades.

drivers.driver: After-close TDnet buybacks, dividend hikes and guidance revisions
drivers.why_today: BayCurrent, TOA, Tokyo Kikai and Caulis all posted clear shareholder-return or earnings catalysts, and those disclosures are supporting the few stocks still trading against the tape.

headline_inventory.headline: Reuters' New York market summary showed a 768-point Dow drop, USD/JPY around 159.7, the U.S. 10-year at 4.261%, and WTI moving back above $100 after hours
FISCO's March 18 New York market wrap showed Chicago Nikkei 225 futures at 53,465, down 1,445 versus Osaka day session
At 11:00 JST USD/JPY was 159.66, up 0.91 yen versus the previous evening's level
One-week USD/JPY implied volatility climbed into the 11% range, showing stronger concern over intervention and the BOJ event
At 11:00 JST the Nikkei was 53,744.86, down 1,494.54, while Prime breadth stayed overwhelmingly negative at 42 gainers versus 1,529 losers
Only mining and shipping were positive among the 33 Prime sectors, leaving resources and shipping as the market's relative-strength pockets
Micron beat revenue expectations on guidance, but the stock still fell about 4% after hours as higher capex overshadowed the sales outlook
INPEX extended its record-high run, with WTI briefly reaching $100.55 in after-hours trade
BayCurrent disclosed a buyback capped at 4.3% of shares outstanding and the full cancellation of acquired shares
TOA raised its full-year outlook and year-end dividend forecast
Tokyo Kikai sharply raised its FY March 2026 net-profit outlook after booking litigation-related income
Caulis approved a buyback capped at 4.14% of shares outstanding and 300 million yen

watchlist.name: BayCurrent
watchlist.why_today: It disclosed a buyback capped at 4.3% plus full cancellation of acquired shares. Even in a broad selloff, it remains near the top of Prime gainers, making the catalyst easy to validate.
watchpoints: Can it hold the upper 4,600 yen area with real dip-buying volume
Can it stay near the top of Prime gainers after the lunch break
Does buying remain even in a BOJ-waiting market
falsifiers: The index selloff deepens enough to erase the relative-strength profile
The stock slips below the 4,600 yen area and the move ends as an opening burst only
watchlist.red_team: The repurchase begins in mid-April, so today's move is still exposed to expectation-led reversal risk.

watchlist.name: TOA
watchlist.why_today: It raised full-year guidance and the year-end dividend after the close. Even in a weak tape, the stock is still higher, making the number-driven thesis easier to test into the afternoon.
watchpoints: Can it hold the 1,800 yen area
Does the market keep rewarding the higher net profit and dividend more than the flat operating-profit line
Can it stay firm with turnover even at higher levels
falsifiers: The market decides the earnings quality is weaker than it first appeared
Selling takes over after lunch and forces the stock back into the red
watchlist.red_team: Tax credits and received-dividend gains mattered, so investors may keep questioning the quality of the upgrade.

sources.title: ＴＯＡ(6809) 業績予想の修正および期末配当予想の修正（増配）に関するお知らせ

watchlist.name: INPEX
watchlist.why_today: WTI briefly reached $100.55 after hours and USD/JPY stayed at 159.66. In a broad selloff, it remains a clean read on resource-sector relative strength.
watchpoints: Does the crude strength hold during Tokyo trading hours
Can the stock stay relatively firm against the Nikkei selloff
Does the bid spread to other energy names such as JAPEX
falsifiers: Crude reverses sharply lower and the resource premium fades
A post-BOJ yen rebound strips away the external macro tailwind
watchlist.red_team: If the broad selloff intensifies, even commodity beneficiaries can get dragged down with the tape.

sources.title: ＩＮＰＥＸが逆行高で連日の最高値更新、時間外でＷＴＩ価格は一時１００ドル乗せ◇
ＮＹ市場サマリー（18日）ダウ急反落768ドル安、ドル・利回り上昇

watchlist.name: Tokyo Kikai Seisakusho
watchlist.why_today: Litigation-related income drove a sharp increase in the net-profit outlook. The stock briefly reached limit-up territory even in a weak market, keeping it high on the afternoon watchlist.
watchpoints: Can it keep absorbing selling near the highs
Does the market keep prioritizing the net-profit impact despite the one-off nature of the gain
Does turnover stay active enough for the move to survive into the afternoon
falsifiers: The market starts focusing on the one-off nature of the gain and the stock reverses sharply
More than half of the early gain is lost after a new intraday high
watchlist.red_team: Because the move is centered on special profit, repeatability is weak and price action can become pure volatility.

sources.title: 東京機(6335) 業績予想の修正に関するお知らせ
東京機が一時Ｓ高、訴訟関連収入の計上で２６年３月期最終利益予想を上方修正

watchlist.name: Caulis
watchlist.why_today: It announced a buyback capped at 4.14% of shares outstanding and 300 million yen. Even as a small-cap name, the reaction was clearly positive, so the afternoon follow-through is worth checking.
watchpoints: Can it hold around the 1,100 yen area
Does turnover stay alive despite the smaller-cap profile
Does fresh chasing demand appear after the buyback-driven rebound
falsifiers: Small-cap profit-taking rapidly erases the gain
The market judges the buyback size to be too small
watchlist.red_team: The catalyst is clear, but thin liquidity can make the move unstable very quickly.

deep_dive.title: BayCurrent: In a broad selloff, test whether the shareholder-return reset still leads

lines: When more than 90% of the market is down, supply-improvement catalysts can have more immediate force than ordinary earnings momentum.
A buyback capped at 4.3% plus full cancellation of acquired shares is a simple and powerful relative-strength setup even in a weak tape.
After the March 18 close, BayCurrent disclosed the buyback and cancellation plan, and Reuters reported on March 19 morning that it was the top Prime-market gainer.
Even with the Nikkei down 1,494 points at 11:00 JST, BayCurrent remained one of the very few positive contributors.
Can it hold the upper 4,600 yen area rather than fade after the opening burst.
After lunch, does dip-buying keep showing up with real volume so that the stock stays near the top of the gainers list.
Broader risk-off selling intensifies and wipes out the relative-strength profile.
The stock breaks below the 4,600 yen area and the post-buyback move clearly fades.
The repurchase starts on April 15, so today's move is still partly expectation-led and vulnerable to reversal.
BOJ headlines or a sharp FX move can quickly push macro conditions ahead of shareholder-return logic.

no_trade_conditions: If the BOJ decision or Governor Ueda's press conference sharply reverses USD/JPY and changes the market's morning leadership
If WTI loses most of its after-hours rebound and resource-sector relative strength breaks down
If BayCurrent or TOA cannot hold their morning high zones and their relative strength disappears
If the broad selloff deepens and index pressure starts dominating stock-specific catalysts
